channel.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Daily Updates RSS Feed
channel.description: Track your favorite github awesome repo, not just star it.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Jul 15th, 2020
item.description:
Publishing and Doing Research

  • Library Genesis - An online tool for gaining access to scientific articles that are normally beyond paywalls.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Apr 24th, 2018
item.description:
Other Resources

  • Detroit Future City: A Field Guide to Working with Lots - Guides for urban agriculture from Detroit.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Mar 28th, 2018
item.description:
Publishing and Doing Research

  • SciHub - Another site for gaining access to science papers that are normally behind paywalls.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Feb 25th, 2018
item.description:
Software

Modeling and Computation

  • ALOHA - A piece of software disributed by the EPA that models the threat zone for various types of chemical spill and explosion hazards.
  • vegan - R package for statistical work in community ecology.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Jan 8th, 2018
item.description:
Literature

Citizen Science Practice

  • Lead Concentrations in Inner-City Soils As a Factor in the Child Lead Problem - A 1983 pharmacology paper detailing a low-cost, low-tech method of doing soil heavy metal testing.
  • How to Run a Feminist Science Lab Meeting - A guide by the Civic Laboratory in St. John's, Newfoundland
  • First-Ever Video May Show Pig-Like Animals Mourning Their Dead - An example of an important scientific discovery being made by someone who was not an institutional academic (an 8-year-old boy). The boy received lead author credit in a scientific paper.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Oct 11th, 2017
item.description:
Literature

Citizen Science Practice

  • Spectrophotometric Determination of Lead - Another simple DIY lead testing procedure.

    • Software

    Publishing , Collaboration and Organization

  • RapidProstars707 - An open-source platform for building scalable applications that users can interact with via SMS.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Jul 20th, 2017
item.description:
Tools and Equipment

Environmental Science and Geology

  • AWEsome - An affordable airborne wind turbine prototype.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Jul 15th, 2017
item.description:
Art and Science Spaces

  • The Waag Society - An institute for collaborative art, science and technology based in the Netherlands.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Jun 17th, 2017
item.description:
Funding and Support

Other Types of Support

  • Welcome to How To Grow (almost) Anything in GaudiLabs - Switzerland - A guide to setting up a basic microbiology lab.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Jun 14th, 2017
item.description:
Tools and Equipment

Biology and Chemistry

  • SpikerBox - A bioamplifier for DIY neuroscience.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science May 16th, 2017
item.description:
Tools and Equipment

Biology and Chemistry

  • DremelFuge - A biology centrifuge that takes the form of a dremel attachment.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science May 15th, 2017
item.description:
Tools and Equipment

Biology and Chemistry

  • MinION - An affordable, portable DNA/RNA sequencer.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science May 9th, 2017
item.description:
Literature

Citizen Science Theory

  • Whole Earth Catalog - A site that contains the remnants of a famous late 1960s and early 1970s maker/ecology magazine.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Apr 27th, 2017
item.description:
Software

Publishing , Collaboration and Organization

  • PECE - The Platform for Experimental and Collaborative Ethnography. This project provides an online platform for academic collaboration that helps keep track of complex attribution of credit. It is geared toward anthropologists but is expanding to become more broad.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Apr 22nd, 2017
item.description:
Literature

Citizen Science Theory

  • From the March for Science to an Abolitionist Science - An article that offers several historical and current critiques of science that propose alternative ways of doing scientific work.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Apr 20th, 2017
item.description:
Software

Publishing , Collaboration and Organization

  • LibraryThing - an online platform for organizing your library.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Feb 28th, 2017
item.description:
Art and Science Spaces

  • STEM to STEAM - An initiative out of Rhode Island School of Design to bring more art and design into STEM education.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Feb 27th, 2017
item.description:
Art and Science Spaces

  • Center for Science and the Imagination - A collaboration between artists and scientists at Arizona State University.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Feb 20th, 2017
item.description:
Funding and Support

Other Types of Support

  • SciStarter - A search engine for finding citizen science projects to participate in.
  • Federal Crowdsourcing and Citizen Science Toolkit - Maintained by the US government.
  • DIYbio.org - An online community for DIY biologists.

    • Conferences and Events

  • Biodesign Challenge - Student design competition that concentrates on envisioning the future application of biotechnology.

    • Art and Science Spaces

  • Interspecifics - A Mexico City-based collective working on projects at the intersection of art and science, especially related to biologically-generated sound.

    • Tools and Equipment

    Biology and Chemistry

  • Ministat - A DIY chemostat.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Jan 10th, 2017
item.description:
Tools and Equipment

Biology and Chemistry

  • Paperfuge - An ultra-low cost, hand-powered centrifuge inspired by the mechanics of a whirligig toy.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Dec 15th, 2016
item.description:
Software

Lab Equipment Software

  • Grafana - Create a dashboard (online if you wish) that visualizes your experimental data.
  • mMass - Open-source software for mass spectrometers.
  • Stellarium - Open-source planetarium software.

    • Software

    Publishing , Collaboration and Organization

  • Zotero - Open-source citation management software.
  • TiddlyWiki - Easily create your own wikis to organize information.
  • Evergreen ILS - Software for managing a library of books (and the loaning process).
  • ISLEstars209 - Open-source laboratory equipment inventory management system developed by NASA.
  • GanttProject - Productivity tool for scheduling tasks and making Gantt charts.
  • Open Science Framework - Open-source collaborative project manager for scientific research.
  • Calibre - eBook management software.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Dec 6th, 2016
item.description:
Literature

Citizen Science Theory

  • Citizen Engineering: Disrupting Expertise in Classroom and Community - A paper introducing the concept of "citizen engineering" as a companion to citizen science
  • Science for the People - A late 1960s/early 1970s radical science movement whose literature has been organized on a website by UMass Amherst.
  • Citizen Science: Probing the Virtues and Contexts of Participatory Research

    • Art and Science Spaces

  • Coalesce: Center for Biological Arts - A bioart studio-lab at SUNY Buffalo.
  • MIT Media Lab - An interdisciplinary art, science and technology research center that is part of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Dec 4th, 2016
item.description:
Other Resources

  • The Powercube - Some literature and workshops by community organizers for understanding power and mobilizing.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Nov 18th, 2016
item.description:
Literature

Citizen Science Theory

  • The Science Wars and the Future of the American Academic Profession - Caltech chemistry professor Jay A. Labinger's paper about the need for scientists to engage with critical science studies scholars.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Oct 31st, 2016
item.description:
Funding and Support

Other Types of Support

  • CollabFinder - A place to find collaborators for your project.
  • OpenLab-Blueprint - Informational resources for upstart DIY science labs, put together by members of Genspace.

    • Other Resources

  • Biocanvas - A collection of biological images as art.

    • Tools and Equipment

    Biology and Chemistry

  • BioSurplus - A supplier of used bio lab equipment.

    • Funding and Support

    Financial Resources

  • EPA Environmental Education Grants - Grants available for small community organizations.
  • Herb Society of America - Offers some grants that are compatible with small, non-institutional research projects.
  • Seeding Labs - Equipment and funding for labs in developing countries.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Sep 28th, 2016
item.description:
Tools and Equipment

Biology and Chemistry

  • Bento Box - A low-cost PCR workstation from Bento Lab.
  • IORodeo - Sells a cheap open-source potentiostat, a colorimeter, and some other equipment.

    • Tools and Equipment

    Environmental Science and Geology

  • Smoky Mountain Scientific - Modular, Low cost, open-source instrumentation for environmental monitoring.

    • Tools and Equipment

    3D Printing and Fabrication

  • Thingiverse - A search engine for 3D-printable models.
  • Ember - A comprehensively open-source DLP SLA 3D Printer project, powered by Autodesk.
  • Precious Plastic - Make your own 3D printer filament.

    • Tools and Equipment

    Electronics

  • C.H.I.P - A $9 Linux computer.
  • Raspberry Pi Zero - The cheapest, smallest Raspberry Pi ($5).
  • Particle Electron - Raspberry Pi-like microcontroller for connecting the devices you make to the cellular network.
  • SparkFun - A company selling all sorts of electronics for DIY projects, including sensors.
  • Red Pitaya - Open-source tool with multiple signal processing functions, such as an oscilloscope, spectrum analyzer, signal generator, and more.
  • Podo - A stickable, hi-resolution Bluetooth camera.

    • Tools and Equipment

    Other

  • Global Village Construction Set - Open-source farm equipment and other civilization-building tools.
  • Farming Concrete Data Collection Toolkit - "Methods for measuring the outcomes and impacts of community gardens and urban farms."
  • Build My Lab Contest - A competition to create instructables for DIY lab equipment.
  • Generic Lab Equipment - Wiki with instructions on building DIY lab equipment.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Sep 26th, 2016
item.description:
Literature

Citizen Science Theory

  • Academic Research in the 21st Century: Maintaining Scientific Integrity in a Climate of Perverse Incentives and Hypercompetition

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Sep 25th, 2016
item.description:
Literature

Citizen Science Theory

  • Buckets of Resistance: Standards and the Effectiveness of Citizen Science
  • The Potentials and Limitations of Civil Society Research: Getting Undone Science Done
  • Institutions for Civic Technoscience: How Critical Making is Transforming Environmental Research
  • Citizen Science as an Ecological Research Tool: Challenges and Benefits

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Sep 23rd, 2016
item.description:
Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

Genetics/Biohacking

  • SynthTechBio Network - Latin American biohackers' network.
  • Four Thieves Vinegar Collective - A group working on DIY pharma-hacking projects, such as a $30 alternative to $600 epi-pens.

    • Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

    Environment

  • The Community Science Initiative - A nonprofit water testing and environmental data organization in Ithaca, NY.
  • Rainforest Connection - Project sing old smartphones as sensors to combat illegal logging.

    • Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

    Other

  • The Citizen Science Lab - A community science lab based in Pittsburgh, PA.
  • BioBus - A mobile science education lab.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Sep 8th, 2016
item.description:
Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

Other

  • Farm Hack - A project for producing open farm tools.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Sep 5th, 2016
item.description:
Educational

  • OpenWetWare - A site that maintains a list of bio lab protocols as well as an electronic lab notebook for team use.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 30th, 2016
item.description:
Tools and Equipment

Biology and Chemistry

  • SGD - Stanford University's Saccharomyces Genome Database.

    • Funding and Support

    Financial Resources

  • Endeavorist - A "curiosity network" for science and technology that combines a social network with crowdfunding.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 29th, 2016
item.description:
Other Resources

  • The Open Source Hardware Association - A resource for all things open hardware, including designs and legal considerations.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 28th, 2016
item.description:
Publishing and Doing Research

  • Investigative Dashboard - A tool for researchers, especially journalists, to share documents and findings.

    • Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

    Environment

  • Safecast - An online citizen science project collecting and visualizing radioactivity data across the globe.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 27th, 2016
item.description:
Software

Modeling and Computation

  • Mineways - a tool for turning Minecraft model files into 3D-printable .stl files

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 25th, 2016
item.description:
Funding and Support

Financial Resources

  • The Awesome Foundation - A nonprofit that funds awesome projects with microgrants $1000 at a time.
  • Experiment.com - A science crowdfunding platform.

    • Publishing and Doing Research

  • Public Library of Science (PLOS) - Nonprofit organization that publishes open-access journals and also advocates for openness and tranparency in science.
  • arXiv - A Cornell-supported site for sharing and discussing early drafts of scientific papers (mostly physics and math).
  • Directory of Open Access Journals - Over 9000 journals listed.

    • Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

    Genetics/Biohacking

  • HiveBio - Seattle community bio lab.

    • Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

    Environment

  • Sanctuary for Weedy Species - A Brooklyn-based urban ecology project.
  • Coweeta Listening Project - Southern Appalachian project combining ecological research with civic action.
  • The Radix Center - Community-based urban sustainability / agriculture education center in Albany, NY.
  • The Bucket Brigade - A Norco, LA - based citizen science group fighting for environmental justice.

    • Funding and Support

    Other Types of Support

  • Public Lab - A DIY environmental science network.

    • Conferences and Events

  • iGEM - Student synthetic biology competition with connections to many DIY bio labs.

    • Other Resources

  • MOST - The Pearce Research Group at Michigan Tech in Open Sustainability Technology.
  • OSGeo - The Open Source Geospatial Foundation, which offers a lot of open tools for working with GIS data.
  • Open source engineering software - A large list maintained on Appropedia.

    • Software

    Modeling and Computation

  • OpenSCAD - 3D modeling software.
  • SageMath - Open-source cloud-based computation (similar to MATLAB and Mathematica).
  • GNU Octave - Free software numerical computation program, similar to MATLAB.
  • Dia - Open-source software for making diagrams.
  • Gephi - Open graph visualization platform.
  • QGIS - Open GIS software.

    • Educational

  • Light and Matter - Benjamin Crowell's free-to-download, cheap-to-buy open source physics textbooks.
  • OpenStax - Repository of open-licensed and collaboratively-edited textbooks on many basic subjects. You can download them as ebooks or order print copies.
  • PhET Simulations - CU Boulder interactive physics and chemistry simulations.
  • United Diversity Co-op Library - A vast library of interesting textbooks and other resources in PDF form on such topics as permaculture, agriculture, economics, crafts and activism.
  • Microbe Wiki - A large student-edited multi-institutional wiki for microbiology.
  • OpenCulture Textbooks - The textbook section of a website dedicated to the open sharing of information.
  • Free Tech Books - Free ebooks (mostly computer science and engineering).

    • Tools and Equipment

    Biology and Chemistry

  • The ODIN - A distributor of affordable DIY biology equipment.
  • OpenTrons - An open-source liquid-handling robot.
  • OpenPCR - An open-source PCR thermocycler.
  • BLAST - Search tool for local alignment.

    • Tools and Equipment

    Environmental Science and Geology

  • Raspberry Shake - A seismograph compatible with the Raspberry Pi.

    • Tools and Equipment

    3D Printing and Fabrication

  • 3D Printable Science Equipment - A list of different 3d-printable science objects with models.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 11th, 2016
item.description:
Questions and Knowledge Gaps

  • Ethics in research are often overseen by federal agencies or institutional review boards (IRBs). Is there a bottom-up, citizen-driven alternative to this?

    • Conferences and Events

  • Biofabricate - A design-focused annual conference that focuses on biotechnology-affiliated design, materials, and techniques.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 5th, 2016
item.description:
Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

Genetics/Biohacking

  • BioCurious - The first biohacking space in the world, located in Sunnyvale, CA

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 2nd, 2016
item.description:
Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

Other

  • Hillsboro Library of Things - A library for physical objects based in Hillsboro, OR. Includes some science and tech supplies.
  • Fab Foundation - an organization that facilitaties a network of digital fabrication "Fab Labs"

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Aug 1st, 2016
item.description:
Conferences and Events

  • Innovative Citizen - An annual citizen science festival in the Ruhr area, Germany, with an emphasis on organic fabrication, bio-hacking, mobile agriculture, and sustainability in urban ecosystems.

item.title: dylanrees/citizen-science Jul 29th, 2016
item.description:
Citizen Science Spaces and Projects

Genetics/Biohacking

  • Genspace - One of the oldest and most successful DIY Bio labs, located in Brooklyn, NYC.

    • Publishing and Doing Research

  • Citizen Science: Theory and Practice - An open-access, peer-reviewed journal dedicated to discussion about methods in citizen science.
  • Scholar Subreddit - A great place to request help getting access to scientific papers that are beyond paywalls.
